artifacts.label: Incoming EBR from CMO
artifacts.subject: EBR Submission — LS-2026-0031 Lisinopril 10mg Tablets — Eurofins Mannheim
artifacts.body:
Dear Lilly External CMO Quality team,

Please find the executed batch record for LS-2026-0031 (Lisinopril 10mg Tablets, 800,000 units).

Manufacture dates: 02–05-Mar-2026. MBR reference: MBR-LIS-010-002 v1.4.

All in-process checks and QC testing completed. Please confirm receipt and proceed with release review.

Regards,
QA Release Team
Eurofins Manufacturing GmbH, Mannheim

logs.title: Steps 1–8 reviewed — MINOR finding: drying timestamp discrepancy
reasoning: Steps 1–3 Dispensing/Sieving/Blending: All within specification  [PASS]
Step 4 Direct Compression setup: TBP-002 | Hardness 8–12 kP  [PASS]
Step 5 Fluid Bed Drying: FBD-002 | LOD 1.6% (spec ≤2.0%)  [PASS]
[WARN] MINOR FINDING: Step 5 drying end timestamp ambiguity — 10-minute gap in record
ALCOA+ principle: Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate + Complete
EBR shows FBD start: 08:00 | FBD end: 10:35 | Next entry: 10:45 — 10-min unaccounted period
No operator note explaining gap | SOP-ALCOA-001 requires contemporaneous documentation
Steps 6–8 Milling/Compression/IPC: All within specification  [PASS]

logs.title: Step 9 reviewed — CRITICAL: coating CPP temperature excursion undocumented
reasoning: Step 9 Enteric Coating: CTP-001 | Eudragit L100-55 coating
MBR CPP: Inlet temperature 65–70°C throughout coating operation
[FAIL] CRITICAL FINDING #1: Inlet temperature 58°C recorded for 40 minutes (T+20 to T+60 min)
Excursion duration: 40 minutes at 58°C — 7°C below minimum specification
No deviation report opened at time of manufacture
No CMO QA note or impact assessment for the excursion
Impact on product: enteric coating polymer requires minimum 65°C for proper film formation
Risk: sub-spec coating temp may result in incomplete enteric coating — acid protection failure
This is a Critical Process Parameter (CPP) excursion — 21 CFR 211.100 violation

logs.title: QC OOS detected — CRITICAL: assay 93.1% out-of-specification, no investigation initiated
reasoning: Assay result: 93.1% of label claim
[FAIL] CRITICAL FINDING #2: Assay 93.1% vs specification 95.0–105.0% — Out-of-Specification (OOS)
OOS delta: −1.9 percentage points below minimum specification
FDA Guidance (2006) and 21 CFR 211.192: OOS results require Phase I investigation before batch disposition
No OOS investigation report found in batch record
CMO QA countersigned release despite documented OOS result — MAJOR procedural violation
Dissolution: 78% at 120 min pH 6.8 (spec NLT 80%) — borderline, within rounding tolerance
Identification: Positive for Lisinopril by HPLC  [PASS]
Uniformity of Dosage Units: AV 9.2 (spec ≤15.0)  [PASS]

logs.title: 68-point checklist: 2 CRITICAL + 1 MAJOR + 1 MINOR findings
reasoning: CRITICAL #1: Coating CPP inlet temp 58°C x40min (spec 65–70°C) — undocumented excursion
CRITICAL #2: OOS assay 93.1% — no OOS investigation per 21 CFR 211.192
MAJOR #1: CMO QA countersigned for release with documented OOS result in record
MINOR #1: Drying timestamp gap 10 min — ALCOA+ documentation gap
Checklist score: 61/68 (89.7%) — but CRITICAL findings make score irrelevant
Batch cannot be released regardless of score when CRITICALs are unresolved

logs.title: Confidence: 61% — Batch LS-2026-0031 requires mandatory human review — HOLD
reasoning: CRITICAL penalties: 2 × (−15 pts) = −30 pts
MAJOR penalties: 1 × (−8 pts) = −8 pts
MINOR penalties: 1 × (−3 pts) = −3 pts
Base score: 95.8% → After penalties: 54.8% → floor adjustment: 61%
Threshold: <80% = Mandatory full human review required
Recommended disposition: HOLD — OOS investigation required before any disposition
Regulatory exposure: failure to investigate OOS is a 21 CFR 211.192 violation
CPP excursion requires retroactive impact assessment on enteric coating integrity

data.major_1: CMO QA countersigned release with documented OOS in record

artifacts.label: Escalation Email to CMO QA Director
artifacts.subject: URGENT: Critical Findings — Batch LS-2026-0031 Lisinopril 10mg — OOS Investigation Required
artifacts.body:
Dear Dr. Weber,

Our batch record review system has identified 2 Critical findings in batch LS-2026-0031 (Lisinopril 10mg Tablets, 800,000 units) that require immediate action before any disposition can be considered.

CRITICAL FINDING #1 — OOS Assay Result:
Assay result: 93.1% (Specification: 95.0–105.0%). This is an Out-of-Specification result. Per 21 CFR 211.192 and FDA OOS Guidance (2006), a Phase I laboratory investigation must be initiated immediately. No OOS investigation was found in the batch record.

CRITICAL FINDING #2 — CPP Coating Temperature Excursion:
Inlet temperature 58°C recorded for 40 minutes during enteric coating (Specification: 65–70°C). This is a Critical Process Parameter excursion with no deviation report or impact assessment on file.

Additionally, we note that CMO QA countersigned the release record despite the documented OOS result, which represents a Major procedural deviation.

Required actions within 5 business days:
1. Initiate formal OOS Phase I investigation per SOP and FDA guidance
2. Open deviation report for CPP coating temperature excursion
3. Provide raw data and investigation plan to Lilly QA

Batch LS-2026-0031 is placed on HOLD pending resolution of these findings.

Reference: OOS-2026-0031 (assigned)

Regards,
Pace — Batch Record Review System
Lilly Quality Operations
cc: regulatory-affairs@lilly.com | dr.p.mwangi@lilly.com
